description: Application policy used by OK VM Proctoring validation. Entries in banned-programs.json are blocking matches. Applications not matching that catalog are allowed unless a separate customer rule is provided.

decisions.appliesWhen: A running process name, process token, command, path token or macOS .app name matches a normalized catalog entry.
No normalized process candidate matches banned-programs.json.
Guest Additions processes are visible but are not present in banned-programs.json. They remain allowed because desktop usability requires Guest Additions.
A detector checks raw PCI IDs, ACPI internals, CPUID, timing artifacts, Guest Additions files, drivers or services instead of the agreed systeminformation/process/hook scope.
